title: Anbe Anbe Kolladhe Lyrics Jeans
stanzas:
anbe anbe kolladhe..
kanne kannai killaadhe..
penne punnagaiyil
idhayathai vedikaadhe
aiyo unnasaivil uyirai kudikaadhe

penne unadhu mellidai paarthen
adadaa brahman kanjanadi
sattre nimirndhen thalai suttri ponen
aahaa avane vallaladi..

minnalai pidithu thoorigai samaithu
ravivarman ezhuthiya vathanamadi
nooradi padhukkai
aaradi aakki
sirpigal sethukkiya uruvamadi

ithuvarai mannil pirandha pennil
neethaan neethaan azhagiyadi
ithanai azhagum motham serthu
ennai vadaippathu kodumaiyadi

koduthu vaitha poove poove
aval koondhal manam solvaayaa
koduthu vaitha nathiye nathiye
aval kulitha sugam solvaayaa
koduthu vaitha koluse
kaalalavai solvaayaa
koduthu vaitha maniye
maarazhagai solvaayaa

azhagiya nilavil oxygen nirappi
ange unakkoru veedu seiven
unnuyir kaakka ennuyir kondu
uyirukku uyiraai thunai iruppaen

megathai pidithu methaigal amaithu
melliya poo unnai thoonga vaippen
thookkathil maarbil verkkindra pothu
natchathiram kondu naan thudaippen

paal vanna paravai kulippatharkaaga
panithuli ellaam segaripen
devathai kulitha thuligalai alli
theertham endrae naan kudippen

anbe anbe kolladhe..
kanne kannai killaadhe..
penne punnagaiyil
idhayathai vedikaadhe
aiyo unnasaivil uyirai kudikaadhe

seo.title: Anbe Anbe Kolladhe Lyrics Jeans

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

The song expresses deep romantic feelings and the pain of love, beautifully conveyed through its lyrics.

What is the context of this song in the movie?

In Jeans, this song highlights the romantic relationship between the lead characters, showcasing their emotional connection.

Who is the singer and music director of this song?

The song is sung by Hariharan and composed by AR Rahman.

When was the song released?

The song was released on April 24, 1998.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In the movie Jeans, Anbe Anbe Kolladhe serves as a poignant expression of love and longing. The song's romantic theme is beautifully illustrated through its heartfelt lyrics and captivating melody. It evokes a sense of nostalgia and deep emotional connection, making it a favorite among listeners. The combination of Hariharan's soulful voice and AR Rahman's masterful composition creates an unforgettable musical experience.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Anbe Anbe Kolladhe from the film Jeans is a mesmerizing romantic melody that beautifully captures the essence of love. With Hariharan's soulful vocals and AR Rahman's enchanting composition, this song transports listeners to a world of deep emotions and longing. The poetic lyrics by Vairamuthu add a layer of depth, making it an unforgettable classic.
stanzaMeanings: Oh my love, don't take away your eyes, girl, don't let your heart be troubled by a smile, oh, don't let your life be taken away by your words.
I looked at your gentle face, oh, I became like a Brahmin, I turned my head around, oh, he is a generous one.
I captured the lightning and created a storm, the words written by Ravi Varma, I turned them into a hundred beds, and the sculptures were shaped beautifully.
Until now, among all the girls born in this world, you are the one, you are the beautiful one, all this beauty combined is a great burden for me.
Oh my love, don't take away your eyes, girl, don't let your heart be troubled by a smile, oh, don't let your life be taken away by your words.

The flower that was given, will it speak of her curls? The river that was given, will it speak of her happiness? The pot that was given, will it speak of her beauty? The jewel that was given, will it speak of her charm?
In the beautiful moonlight, I will create a home for you, I will protect your life with my life, I will be a companion for your life.
I captured the clouds and calmed the storms, I will let you sleep, when the stars are shining, I will start my journey with them.
To bathe the bird that is the color of milk, I will gather all the dew, if I say that I will offer the holy water to the goddess, I will drink it.
Oh my love, don't take away your eyes, girl, don't let your heart be troubled by a smile, oh, don't let your life be taken away by your words.
